description: PCL-5 clearly elevated (52 of 80), late-identified woman in her late 30s processing a past event with classic intrusion, avoidance, and hypervigilance patterns. Baseline trauma-path interpretation.

mustMention: intrusion or avoidance or hypervigilance (the actual symptom texture)
evidence-based modalities (EMDR, somatic experiencing, IFS, or TF-CBT)
trauma responses are adaptations, not failures
mustAvoid: you just need to move on
expectedQualities.notes: Should engage the specific symptom texture from intake (intrusive memories, hypervigilance, the body's altered baseline), hold diagnostic line, recommend trauma-specialized clinician with specific credentials worth looking for, suggest authors from the whitelist (van der Kolk, Herman, Foo), close with care.

intake.intakeFreeText: Something happened a few years ago that I have never really talked about. I thought I had dealt with it. But it keeps coming back. I cannot watch certain movies. I jump at loud noises. I avoid the part of town where it happened. I sleep badly and wake up with my heart racing. I have started to feel like the world is fundamentally dangerous in a way that I never used to. I do not know if this counts as trauma in the clinical sense or if I am being dramatic, but my partner suggested I look into it.

screeners.cutoffMeaning: A total score of 33 or above is indicative of probable PTSD and warrants further assessment by a qualified clinician.
